scenes.voiceover: You use credit cards for everything.
You'll pay it off later, but the balance keeps growing.
Minimum payments feel manageable.
But interest adds up fast.
A $3,000 balance at 18% interest takes years to pay off with minimums.
You pay hundreds or thousands in interest alone.
Before you know it, you're deep in debt.
In America, credit is everywhere.
Easy to get, hard to escape.
Offers arrive constantly.
Buy now, pay later is on every website.
Debt is normalized.
But relying on credit keeps you broke because you're paying for the past instead of building the future.
Every dollar to interest could have gone to savings or investment.
The psychological trap is that credit doesn't feel like real money.
Swiping is painless.
Your brain doesn't register it the same way.
It's abstract.
Future you's problem.
But eventually, future you becomes present you, and the bill is real.
Breaking free starts with stopping the cycle.
Stop using cards.
Pay cash or debit only.
Then make a plan to pay down what you owe.
